Coagulation

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows marked metabolic acidosis (pH 6.9) with preserved pCO₂ and bicarbonate, severe hyperbilirubinemia, elevated creatinine (suggesting renal dysfunction), mild hyperchloremia, prolonged aPTT, and low AST. Liver dysfunction and acute or chronic kidney injury are likely. Immediate clinical assessment is recommended due to life-threatening acidosis and multi-organ involvement.
